trackingsummarydata.subject: This week in A&S: Our top stories of 2025
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 12/15/25

urls.page_title: Celebrating the journey with our December A&S grads
urls.page_summary: December graduates walk the stage this month, so we sat down for a talk with three A&S grads who’ve taken different pathways through Cornell.
urls.page_title: National Science Foundation announces Cornell-led AI Materials Institute
urls.page_summary: The NSF, in partnership with Intel, will invest $20 million over five years.
urls.page_title: Center for Historical Keyboards boosted by $5 million gift
urls.page_summary: The gift will secure the future of the center's museum-quality holdings, as well as a rich program of concerts, festivals and educational offerings.
urls.page_title: A&S launches Cornell in Los Angeles semester study away
urls.page_summary: The program will place students in Hollywood for a semester of coursework, networking, and cultural immersion.
urls.page_title: New course encourages students to embrace disagreement
urls.page_summary: A new course being offered will give students the chance to consider some of the most polarizing issues in our world today.
Innovative and far-reaching discoveries such as better electric batteries, carbon capture technologies, renewable plastics and improvements in solar cells are just a few of their research areas.
urls.page_title: Behavioral economics: Born at Cornell, embraced by policymakers
urls.page_summary: Economists and psychologists work together to understand how human behavior impacts people's decision-making in the marketplace.
urls.page_title: New high-powered telescope reaches Chilean peak
urls.page_summary: The first major component of the Fred Young Submillimeter Telescope (FYST) has arrived at its final home: the Cerro Chajnantor mountaintop.
Author and editor Sam Tanenhaus reminded listeners that politically complex, and even morally ambiguous, material makes for great storytelling.
